key_trials.full_name: First-line Nivolumab Plus Ipilimumab versus Chemotherapy in Patients with Unresectable Malignant Pleural Mesothelioma
key_trials.intervention: Nivolumab (3 mg/kg Q2W) + Ipilimumab (1 mg/kg Q6W) for up to 2 years.
key_trials.comparator: Cisplatin (75 mg/m2) or Carboplatin (AUC 5) + Pemetrexed (500 mg/m2) Q3W for up to 6 cycles.
key_trials.key_result: Median OS was 18.1 months with nivolumab+ipilimumab vs 14.1 months with chemotherapy (HR 0.75; 95% CI 0.64-0.88; p=0.002) at 3-year follow-up [9,148,149].
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: In non-epithelioid histology (n=150), median OS was 18.1 vs 8.8 months (HR 0.48; 95% CI 0.33-0.68). Grade 3-4 treatment-related AEs: 31% vs 32% [148,149].
key_trials.practice_change: Established nivolumab + ipilimumab as a preferred first-line option, particularly for non-epithelioid histology.

key_trials.full_name: Bevacizumab for Newly Diagnosed Pleural Mesothelioma in the Mesothelioma Avastin Cisplatin Pemetrexed Study
key_trials.intervention: Cisplatin (75 mg/m2) + Pemetrexed (500 mg/m2) + Bevacizumab (15 mg/kg) followed by Bevacizumab maintenance.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: More grade 3-4 AEs with bevacizumab (71% vs 62%), including hypertension (23% vs 0%), proteinuria (3% vs 0%), and thrombotic events (6% vs 1%) [6, MS-5].
key_trials.practice_change: Established the addition of bevacizumab to platinum/pemetrexed as a category 1 first-line option.

key_trials.full_name: Nivolumab versus Placebo in Patients with Relapsed Malignant Mesothelioma
key_trials.population: Relapsed malignant mesothelioma after platinum-based chemotherapy.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: Clinical benefit regardless of PD-L1 expression. Serious AEs similar (41% vs 44%) [16, MS-8].
key_trials.practice_change: Established nivolumab as a preferred subsequent therapy option after platinum-based chemotherapy.

key_trials.full_name: Gemcitabine with or without Ramucirumab as Second-line Treatment for Malignant Pleural Mesothelioma
key_trials.population: Malignant pleural mesothelioma after progression on platinum/pemetrexed.
key_trials.intervention: Gemcitabine (1000 mg/m2 D1,8) + Ramucirumab (8 mg/kg Q2W).
key_trials.practice_change: Supported the use of gemcitabine + ramucirumab as a subsequent therapy option.

key_trials.full_name: Pembrolizumab versus Single-agent Chemotherapy for Advanced Pre-treated Malignant Pleural Mesothelioma
key_trials.population: Advanced mesothelioma after progression on platinum-based chemotherapy.
key_trials.comparator: Investigator's choice: Vinorelbine (30 mg/m2 Q1W) or Gemcitabine (1000 mg/m2 D1,8,15 Q4W).
key_trials.key_result: No significant difference in OS (HR 1.12; 95% CI 0.74-1.69; p=0.59) [165, MS-8].
key_trials.practice_change: Based on this and other data, the Panel supports single-agent vinorelbine or gemcitabine over single-agent pembrolizumab as subsequent therapy.

key_trials.full_name: Mesothelioma and Radical Surgery Randomised Feasibility Study
key_trials.population: Resectable mesothelioma, candidates for trimodality therapy.
key_trials.intervention: EPP after 3 cycles of platinum-based chemotherapy.
key_trials.comparator: No EPP after 3 cycles of platinum-based chemotherapy.
key_trials.key_result: 12-month survival rate: 52.2% (EPP) vs 73.1% (no EPP) (HR 1.9; p=0.082). Median survival: 14.4 vs 19.5 months. Perioperative deaths in EPP group (12.5%) [187, MS-9].
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: Lower quality-of-life scores with EPP [187, MS-9].
key_trials.practice_change: Demonstrated high morbidity/mortality of EPP and influenced the paradigm shift away from routine surgery.

key_trials.full_name: Extended Pleurectomy Decortication and Chemotherapy versus Chemotherapy Alone for Pleural Mesothelioma
key_trials.intervention: 2 cycles platinum/pemetrexed, then P/D (or extended P/D), then 2-4 more cycles chemotherapy.
key_trials.comparator: 2 cycles platinum/pemetrexed, then chemotherapy alone (2-4 more cycles).
key_trials.key_result: Median OS was shorter in the surgery group: 19.3 vs 24.8 months (HR 1.28; 95% CI 1.02-1.62; p=0.032) [188, MS-10].
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: HR for OS in epithelioid: 1.12; in non-epithelioid: 2.66. More grade ≥3 AEs and higher costs with surgery [188, MS-10].
key_trials.practice_change: Confirms systemic therapy alone may be superior to systemic therapy plus P/D, reinforcing systemic therapy as the primary modality for most patients.
